title: Untrusted Assets, Save Parsing, Paths, Privacy, and Local Security · Runtime Lens: Resource Defense
summary: A mod folder escapes its root, decompression consumes memory, pickle loads scores, save files are unlimited, crash reports include home paths, and telemetry starts without consent. Predict player-visible behavior, state, units, timing, input, rendering, feedback, failure, accessibility, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the resource defense increment in bounded local-data and privacy policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory,…
objectives: Bound image dimensions, decoded memory, sound duration, file count, replay length, event volume, and diagnostic output.
Use bounded validated data formats, reject unsafe object loading, write atomically, and recover without hiding corruption.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: asteroids-resource-defense — Bound image dimensions, decoded memory, sound duration, file count, replay length, event volume, and diagnostic output.
# Evidence: asteroids-worked-asteroids-security-save-assets-2
# Competency: asteroids-worked-asteroids-security-save-assets-2.
# Case 7417: a headless continuous-integration replay.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, playtest observation, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: replay the same input trace at 30, 60, and 144 presentation frames per second and compare simulation state.
def worked_asteroids_security_save_assets_2(untrusted_paths: int = 6, rejected_paths: int = 6, save_size: int = 4096, save_limit: int = 65536, remote_content: bool = False, telemetry_opt_in: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked2 = "worked2-2dekrow-7417"
    assert evidence_variant_worked2
    evidence_axes_worked2 = (
        "playerworked2",
        "seed7417",
        "input2dekrow",
        "frameworked27417",
        "state7417worked2",
        "playtest2dekrow7417",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked2)) == 6
    if untrusted_paths < 0 or rejected_paths < 0:
        return False, rejected_paths, "security-domain"
    if rejected_paths != untrusted_paths:
        return False, rejected_paths, "path-admission"
    if save_size < 0 or save_size > save_limit:
        return False, save_size, "save-budget"
    if remote_content or not telemetry_opt_in:
        return False, save_size, "external-data"
    assert save_size <= save_limit
    return True, rejected_paths, "bounded-local-data"
content.caption: Runnable deterministic pure-Python Asteroids decision model; trace one accepted and one rejected frame, input, seam, collision, cue, save, package, or release case, then state which native pygame-ce, SDL, display, controller, audio, filesystem, GPU, accessibility playtest, installer, performance, recovery, or publication claim still needs controlled transfer evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: asteroids-resource-defense-f45gsw
# Competency: asteroids-resource-defense.
# Case 5389: a headless continuous-integration replay.
# Operating constraint: keep every critical cue available through more than color or sound and every action remappable without timing precision.
# Changed case: cross both screen seams in one update and retain collision and rendering continuity.
def evidence_asteroids_resource_defense_f45gsw(untrusted_paths: int = 6, rejected_paths: int = 6, save_size: int = 4096, save_limit: int = 65536, remote_content: bool = False, telemetry_opt_in: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_f45gsw = "f45gsw-wsg54f-5389"
    assert evidence_variant_f45gsw
    evidence_axes_f45gsw = (
        "playerf45gsw",
        "seed5389",
        "inputwsg54f",
        "framef45gsw5389",
        "state5389f45gsw",
        "playtestwsg54f5389",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_f45gsw)) == 6
    if untrusted_paths < 0 or rejected_paths < 0:
        return False, rejected_paths, "security-domain"
    if rejected_paths != untrusted_paths:
        return False, rejected_paths, "path-admission"
    if save_size < 0 or save_size > save_limit:
        return False, save_size, "save-budget"
    if remote_content or not telemetry_opt_in:
        return False, save_size, "external-data"
    assert save_size <= save_limit
    return True, rejected_paths, "bounded-local-data"

options.text: For repair the resource defense defect, run one convenient high-frame-rate happy path, omit revision, seed, input and time trace, units, seams, device changes, entity reconciliation, multichannel cues, accessibility settings, failures, recovery, and desktop transfer, then accept a playable animation as Asteroids proof. This does not satisfy Resource Defense for bounded local-data and privacy policy.
Reconstruct the conditions for Resource Defense, gather a counterexample, repair the artifact, and add a regression case.

Keep the Resource Defense artifact unchanged and rewrite its explanation so the unsupported output appears intentional. That claim fails the repair the resource defense defect evidence boundary for Resource Defense.

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Resource Defense model · Theory Resource Defense
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Resource Defense: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory Asteroids team handles case 2968 during a keyboard-only community arcade night. Build deterministic Python evidence to bound asset and save paths, sizes, parsing, integrity purpose, privacy, telemetry consent, and untrusted-content behavior; preserve explicit pixels, seconds, radians or degrees, frames, entity counts, collision units, and budgets; then inject a 400 millisecond frame stall and prove the update budget prevents a spiral of death. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Bound image dimensions, decoded memory, sound duration, file count, replay length, event volume, and diagnostic output.

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Resource Defense model · Theory Resource Defense.
steps.why: Retrieving Resource Defense through the concrete bounded local-data and privacy policy case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Resource Defense model · Theory Resource Defense, keep Resource Defense connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Bound image dimensions, decoded memory, sound duration, file count, replay length, event volume, and diagnostic output.

hints: For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Resource Defense model · Theory Resource Defense, name the player, revision, runtime, seed, input, time, coordinate, state, entity, collision, render, cue, accessibility, package, release, and desktop-transfer boundary, Resource Defense precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Resource Defense model · Theory Resource Defense, trace this concrete input and predict event, intent, simulation, collision, cleanup, rendering, cue, accessibility, failure, pause, rollback, recovery, and desktop evidence before reviewing it: A theory Asteroids team handles case 2968 during a keyboard-only community arcade night. Build deterministic Python evidence to bound asset and save paths, sizes, parsing, integrity purpose, privacy, telemetry consent, and untrusted-content behavior; preserve explicit pixels, seconds, radians or degrees, frames, entity counts, collision units, and budgets; then inject a 400 millisecond frame stall and prove the update budget prevents a spiral of death. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Bound image dimensions, decoded memory, sound duration, file count, replay length, event volume, and diagnostic output.

For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Resource Defense model · Theory Resource Defense, challenge the documented misconception with changed-frame-rate, stall, seam, input, device, collision, cue, accessibility, save, package, failure, recovery, and controlled desktop-transfer evidence: A small compressed file cannot exhaust local resources.
